notes: v0 — synthesized from 8 extracted candidates + corpus pass over 328 KVA explanations. KVA option taxonomy (A=I>II, B=II>I, C=I=II, D=otillräcklig info) is the foundational meta-rule and is NOT cataloged as a trap; it is assumed. Entries are organized roughly: 001–012 = D-svaret / free-parameter traps (highest leverage — students consistently pick A/B/C when D is correct), 013–022 = sign / parity / direction traps, 023–038 = computation-shortcut and domain-formula traps.

entries.pattern_description: Student sees x² = y² (or kvadratlikhet) and assumes x = y, missing that x = -y is equally valid.
entries.why_it_occurs: The natural reading of 'kvadraterna är lika' is 'talen är lika'. Students forget that squaring loses sign, so the constraint splits into two cases: x = y AND x = -y, both consistent with the given info.
entries.common_distractor_signature: Picks C (I = II) when D is correct — sees the algebraic match and stops before checking the negative branch.
entries.countermeasure: Varje gång du tar kvadratrot eller jämför kvadrater: skriv ut BÅDA fallen x = ±y och kolla om båda är förenliga med villkoren. Om ja → svaret är D.

entries.pattern_description: Student treats a sum-equality (e.g. y + z = 0, or x + y = k) as if it pinned down each individual term.
entries.why_it_occurs: Equations 'feel' like they should determine the variables. But one equation in two unknowns only fixes a linear combination — each term is still free along the constraint line.
entries.common_distractor_signature: Picks A or B based on a chosen specific solution (e.g. setting y = -z and assuming z > 0), missing that the sign of the free variable can flip the svaret.
entries.countermeasure: Räkna frihetsgrader: antal okända − antal oberoende ekvationer. Om resultatet > 0 är minst en variabel fri, och då måste du testa minst två tecken-varianter innan du vågar säga A/B/C.

entries.pattern_description: An inequality bounds only ONE of the two compared variables (e.g. 'Clara > 22'), leaving the other in an open interval that straddles the comparison target.
entries.why_it_occurs: Inequalities look like 'enough info' — they constrain something. Students forget that a lower bound on A says nothing about B, and that A's interval may overlap both sides of B.
entries.common_distractor_signature: Picks A or B by anchoring on the bound (e.g. assumes Clara is exactly 22 or just above), missing that the unconstrained variable can be anywhere in its interval — D.
entries.countermeasure: Skriv ut intervallet för varje variabel explicit. Om båda variablernas möjliga intervall överlappar målvärdet → D.

entries.pattern_description: Geometric figure with fixed area OR fixed perimeter, but free shape — student assumes the side lengths are pinned.
entries.why_it_occurs: 'En rektangel med area 24' sounds concrete. But area = base·höjd has infinite (base, höjd) pairs satisfying it; the longest side can range from a minimum (most compact) up to arbitrarily large.
entries.common_distractor_signature: Picks A or B by imagining a 'typical' rectangle (often a square-ish one), missing that the same constraint admits a needle-thin rectangle with very different side lengths.
entries.countermeasure: Vid fixerad area/omkrets: testa två extremfall — den mest kompakta formen (närmast kvadrat/cirkel) OCH en mycket avlång variant. Om verdikten skiljer sig → D.

entries.pattern_description: A free parameter k (often described as 'k ≠ 0' or 'b är ett tal') is multiplied with a variable; the sign and magnitude of k each open distinct cases.
entries.why_it_occurs: 'k ≠ 0' sounds restrictive but is actually very permissive: k can be large, small (0 < k < 1), or negative — and each gives a different relation between x and kx.
entries.common_distractor_signature: Picks A or B by silently assuming k > 1 (the 'normal' case), missing that 0 < k < 1 reverses the magnitude order and k < 0 reverses the sign.
entries.countermeasure: När en fri parameter multipliceras: testa MINST tre värden — k = 2, k = 1/2, k = -1. Om verdikten skiljer sig mellan dem → D.

entries.pattern_description: A two-way table gives row and column totals but no joint distribution — student treats it as fully specified.
entries.why_it_occurs: Marginal totals look like full information. But without at least one inner-cell value (or independence assumption), the joint distribution is free within the marginals.
entries.common_distractor_signature: Picks A/B/C by assuming proportional/uniform distribution across cells, missing that the inner cells can be anywhere within the constraint.
entries.countermeasure: Vid tvåvägs-tabell utan korsinformation: konstruera TVÅ giltiga ifyllnader som ger olika svar på frågan. Lyckas du → D.

entries.pattern_description: Two given equations look distinct but one is a multiple/proportional rewrite of the other — only one independent constraint remains.
entries.why_it_occurs: Two equations 'should' determine two unknowns. Students count the lines on the page rather than checking linear independence; if the second equation is a multiple of the first, you really have a one-equation-two-unknowns system.
entries.common_distractor_signature: Picks A/B/C by solving the system as if it had a unique solution, missing the dependency. Correct answer is usually D.
entries.countermeasure: Innan du löser ett ekvationssystem: dela ekvation 2 med ekvation 1 (eller multiplicera den ena med en konstant) — om de blir identiska har du bara ETT oberoende villkor.
entries.notes: host-ver1 / host-ver2 are sister-exams with identical questions; both count as separate corpus qids per dataset convention.

entries.pattern_description: Geometric problem with a 'distinguishing' feature (e.g. 'rätvinklig triangel', 'likbent') has multiple valid configurations — student commits to one.
entries.why_it_occurs: 'En rätvinklig triangel' sounds like ONE triangle. But the right angle could be at any of three vertices, and 'likbent' has three choices for which side is the base — each configuration gives different angles/sides.
entries.common_distractor_signature: Picks A/B/C by drawing the most symmetric or 'obvious' configuration, missing that other configurations satisfy the same description but yield a different svaret.
entries.countermeasure: Vid geometriska beskrivningar: lista ALLA möjliga konfigurationer av den utmärkande egenskapen (var sitter rätvinkeln? vilken sida är basen?) innan du svarar. Två giltiga config → D.

entries.pattern_description: Equation forces x = y (or x = -y) but does not fix the actual value — student compares I against a concrete number without checking the unknown's range.
entries.why_it_occurs: Once you derive 'x = y' the algebra feels done. But if the quantity in question depends on the magnitude (e.g. 'x' vs '0') and x is still free, the comparison is not determined.
entries.common_distractor_signature: Picks C because the algebraic relation looks like a match, missing that the comparison target (a constant) doesn't get pinned by 'x = y' alone.
entries.countermeasure: Efter att ha löst en relation mellan variabler: fråga 'är värdet låst, eller bara förhållandet?' Om bara förhållandet är låst och kvantiteten beror på det absoluta värdet → D.

entries.pattern_description: Mean (medelvärde) is given — student assumes the individual values are determined.
entries.why_it_occurs: Mean = sum / count, so mean fixes the SUM but not the individual terms. Students treat 'medel = 5' as if every value were 5 (or close).
entries.common_distractor_signature: Picks A/B/C by assuming the mean equals the comparison value, missing that individual terms can be arbitrarily larger/smaller as long as the sum balances.
entries.countermeasure: Skriv 'medelvärde × antal = totalsumma' och fråga: är totalsumman det jag behöver, eller behöver jag en enskild term? Om en enskild term efterfrågas och inga ytterligare villkor finns → D.

entries.pattern_description: Percent constraint (e.g. '40 % är blå') locks the RATIO but not the absolute count — student treats it as concrete numbers.
entries.why_it_occurs: Percent feels like a count. But '40 % blå' is consistent with 4 av 10, 40 av 100, 400 av 1000 — the absolute number is free unless the total is given.
entries.common_distractor_signature: Picks A/B/C by pretending the total equals 100, missing that the comparison may depend on absolute counts the percent doesn't pin.
entries.countermeasure: När bara procent ges utan totalmängd: pröva två totaler (t.ex. 10 och 100). Skiljer sig verdikten → D.

entries.pattern_description: Speed/distance/time problem where one quantity lies in an interval — student grabs an endpoint instead of testing the whole range.
entries.why_it_occurs: Word problems with a hastighetsintervall feel solvable: 'mellan 50 och 60 km/h' is a concrete-sounding range. Students compute with one endpoint and compare; if the target value lies INSIDE the resulting time/distance interval, the svaret is D.
entries.common_distractor_signature: Picks A or B by computing with the midpoint or one endpoint, missing that the other endpoint flips the comparison.
entries.countermeasure: Vid hastighet/tid/sträcka-intervall: räkna båda ändpunkterna. Om målvärdet ligger i intervallet [v_min, v_max] eller [t_min, t_max] → D.

entries.pattern_description: Negative base raised to a power — student ignores parity of the exponent.
entries.why_it_occurs: Magnitude is the salient feature; the sign-flip from odd vs even exponent is easy to forget. Students compute |(-a)^n| and pick A/B without flipping the sign for odd n.
entries.common_distractor_signature: Picks the option as if the result were positive, missing that odd n preserves the negative sign — flipping the magnitude comparison.
entries.countermeasure: Vid (-a)^n: bestäm tecknet FÖRST via paritet (n udda → negativt, n jämn → positivt), magnituden SEDAN. Aldrig tvärtom.

entries.pattern_description: Multiplying or dividing an inequality by a variable expression with unknown sign — student keeps the direction.
entries.why_it_occurs: Inequalities feel like equations during algebraic manipulation. Students multiply both sides by y (or 1/y) without first checking sign(y); if y could be negative, the inequality flips.
entries.common_distractor_signature: Picks A or B by treating the inequality as preserved through the operation, missing that the answer depends on sign(y) — actual svaret often D.
entries.countermeasure: Innan du multiplicerar/delar en olikhet med ett uttryck: är tecknet känt? Om nej, bryt i fall (positivt / negativt) — om verdikten skiljer sig → D.

entries.pattern_description: Comparing two negative numbers — student picks the one with larger absolute value, forgetting that 'större' means closer to zero.
entries.why_it_occurs: On the number line, 'bigger' and 'further from zero' usually match. For negatives they don't: -3 < -2 even though |-3| > |-2|.
entries.common_distractor_signature: Picks A or B by reading absolute value as magnitude, missing the sign flip — answers exactly inverted.
entries.countermeasure: För negativa tal: större = närmare noll. Rita en tallinje om du är osäker; 'större' = längre till höger, ALLTID.

entries.pattern_description: Slope k and y-y-skärning m are independent — student infers one from the other.
entries.why_it_occurs: Both k and m describe 'where the line is'. Students conflate 'steep line' with 'high y-y-skärning' or vice versa, treating one as a proxy for the other.
entries.common_distractor_signature: Picks A/B by transferring info about k onto m (or vice versa), e.g. 'positiv lutning' is taken to imply 'positiv m'.
entries.countermeasure: y = kx + m har TVÅ oberoende parametrar. Information om k säger inget om m och tvärtom — markera vilken du faktiskt har innan du svarar.

entries.pattern_description: Perpendicular lines — student uses k₂ = -k₁ instead of k₂ = -1/k₁.
entries.why_it_occurs: 'Vinkelrät' suggests 'motsatt riktning', and the negative sign is right — but the relation is the NEGATIVE RECIPROCAL, not the negative. Students drop the inversion.
entries.common_distractor_signature: Picks the option built on k₂ = -k₁ — wrong magnitude but right sign of relationship, looking superficially correct.
entries.countermeasure: Vinkelräta lutningar: produkten är -1, dvs k₂ = -1/k₁. Aldrig bara -k₁. Memorera: 'NEGATIVA INVERSER, inte motsatser'.

entries.pattern_description: Base of an exponential expression — student forgets that base in (0,1) reverses the size-monotonicity in the exponent.
entries.why_it_occurs: 'Större exponent = större värde' is the rule for base > 1, and students apply it universally. For 0 < bas < 1, the relation reverses.
entries.common_distractor_signature: Picks A/B by trusting that larger exponent gives larger value, missing that for bas < 1 it gives a smaller value.
entries.countermeasure: Innan du jämför a^m vs a^n: lokalisera basen mot 1. Bas > 1 → större exponent större värde. 0 < bas < 1 → omvänt. Bas = 1 → alltid 1.

entries.pattern_description: Difference of two ordered quantities (a − b vs b − a) — student misreads the sign.
entries.why_it_occurs: The two differences are negatives of each other; which is positive depends on which of a, b is larger. Students treat them as equal in magnitude and ignore sign.
entries.common_distractor_signature: Picks C (lika) when the answer is D, by reading 'samma skillnad' as if order didn't matter.
entries.countermeasure: a − b vs b − a är ALDRIG lika (såvida inte a = b). Tecknet beror på vilken är större, och om det är okänt → D.

entries.pattern_description: Cylinder volume πr²h — student treats r and h as equally weighted.
entries.why_it_occurs: Both r and h enter the formula linearly in look. But r is SQUARED, so a small radius change dominates a proportional height change.
entries.common_distractor_signature: Picks A/B by comparing r + h or r × h totals, missing that radius dominates because of the r² factor.
entries.countermeasure: I cylindervolym väger r² alltid tyngre än h. Vid jämförelse: räkna r² explicit och jämför r²h direkt, inte r + h.

entries.pattern_description: Median vs mean — student treats them as interchangeable or assumes one constrains the other.
entries.why_it_occurs: For symmetric small series they often coincide. Students generalize and assume changing one shifts the other.
entries.common_distractor_signature: Picks A/B/C by using median when mean is asked (or vice versa), missing that the two are independent statistics that can move in opposite directions.
entries.countermeasure: Median och medelvärde är OBEROENDE — räkna båda explicit (sortera för median, summa/n för medel) och jämför separat. En kan ändras utan att påverka den andra.

entries.pattern_description: Parabola f(x) = x² + bx + c is symmetric kring x = -b/2 — student compares f(a), f(b) at non-symmetric points without using the axis.
entries.why_it_occurs: Students compute f(a) and f(b) directly, which works but is slow and error-prone. They miss that points equidistant from -b/2 give identical f-values.
entries.common_distractor_signature: Picks A or B by miscomputing one of the values, when the symmetri kring x = -b/2 would give C instantly.
entries.countermeasure: För parabel x² + bx + c: identifiera symmetriaxeln x = -b/2 först. Punkter spegelvänt kring den ger LIKA värden (C).

entries.pattern_description: Fractions with different denominators — student compares numerators or denominators directly without aligning.
entries.why_it_occurs: Looking at '5/7 vs 4/9' the student picks the bigger numerator or smaller denominator without converting to a common base.
entries.common_distractor_signature: Picks A or B based on a single-component heuristic ('större täljare = större bråk' or 'mindre nämnare = större bråk'), missing that both components matter.
entries.countermeasure: Bråk med olika nämnare: gör ALLTID gemensam nämnare (multiplicera nämnarna) och jämför täljare. Aldrig en-komponents-heuristik.

entries.pattern_description: Algebraic expressions with shared structure — student computes both sides numerically instead of factoring/distributing to cancel.
entries.why_it_occurs: Direct evaluation feels safe. But many KVA expressions are designed so that distributing or factoring cancels variables, leaving only the constants to compare — saving time and avoiding arithmetic errors.
entries.common_distractor_signature: Slow path leads to numerical errors that pick A/B when expressions are actually equal (C) or differ by a tiny constant.
entries.countermeasure: Innan du räknar: distribuera båda sidor fullständigt OCH bryt ut gemensamma faktorer. Om variablerna kancellerar, jämför bara konstanterna.

entries.pattern_description: Composite discounts mixing percent and absolute (kr) — student assumes order doesn't matter.
entries.why_it_occurs: Percent operations alone are commutative (10% then 20% = 20% then 10%). Mixing kr-rabatt with %-rabatt breaks commutativity — applying kr first then % differs from % first then kr.
entries.common_distractor_signature: Picks C (lika) when the two orderings actually give different final prices — svaret is A or B depending on direction.
entries.countermeasure: Vid sammansatta rabatter: om någon är i kronor (inte procent), spelar ordningen ALLTID roll — räkna båda ordningarna explicit.

entries.pattern_description: Quantities given in different units — student compares the raw numbers without converting.
entries.why_it_occurs: Numbers look bigger or smaller depending on unit (1000 m vs 1 km vs 100 cm). Students miss that '50 m/s vs 100 km/h' requires conversion before comparing.
entries.common_distractor_signature: Picks A or B based on the raw number, missing that the unit changes the magnitude — actual svaret often the opposite.
entries.countermeasure: Vid jämförelse mellan olika enheter: konvertera ALLTID till samma minsta enhet först. Memorera: m/s → km/h multiplicera med 3,6.

entries.pattern_description: Triangle inequality — student forgets the third side must be in (|a − b|, a + b).
entries.why_it_occurs: Knowing two sides feels like 'almost the whole triangle'. Students compare ranges of the third side incorrectly, often by picking a single 'sensible' value instead of the open interval.
entries.common_distractor_signature: Picks A or B by assuming the third side equals some 'natural' value (like the average), missing that the actual range may straddle the comparison target → D.
entries.countermeasure: Tredje sidan i triangel med två sidor a, b ligger i intervallet (|a-b|, a+b). Jämför mot målvärdet via intervallets ändpunkter.

entries.pattern_description: Probability — student compares without explicitly counting both gynnsamma and totala utfall.
entries.why_it_occurs: Probabilities feel intuitable. Students eyeball 'most likely' instead of computing favorable/total for both options, often missing complementary or combinatorial structure.
entries.common_distractor_signature: Picks A/B by intuition, missing that the explicit count gives the opposite svaret — or picks C without checking the exact numbers.
entries.countermeasure: Sannolikhet = gynnsamma / totala. Räkna BÅDA explicit för varje sida innan du jämför — använd komplementhändelse (1 − P(motsatsen)) om direkt räkning är svår.

entries.pattern_description: Identical numerator, different denominator — student fails to apply 'mindre nämnare = större bråk'.
entries.why_it_occurs: When the numerators look harmlessly equal (e.g. 93/70 vs 93/71), students still convert mentally to decimals or estimate, introducing arithmetic errors.
entries.common_distractor_signature: Picks C (lika) by glancing at the same numerator, or picks the wrong direction by confusing nämnare-storlek with bråk-storlek.
entries.countermeasure: Lika täljare ⇒ den med MINDRE nämnare är större. Memorera regeln; pröva inte att räkna ut bråken numeriskt.

entries.pattern_description: Speed = distance / time — student compares speeds without normalizing the time bases.
entries.why_it_occurs: When 'Pia cyklade dubbelt så långt på dubbelt så lång tid', the student assumes she's twice as fast (or slow). The ratio matters, not the absolutes.
entries.common_distractor_signature: Picks A or B by reading distance OR time alone, missing that proportional scaling of both leaves the speed unchanged (C).
entries.countermeasure: Medelhastighet är förhållandet sträcka/tid. Om båda skalas med samma faktor ändras INTE hastigheten. Reducera kvoterna eller korsmultiplicera.

entries.pattern_description: Age problem with multiple people — student gets lost in 'tidigare/senare' time-shifts.
entries.why_it_occurs: Age problems compress multiple time points and relations. Students mix 'ålder då' with 'ålder nu' and lose track of which Δ applies to whom.
entries.common_distractor_signature: Picks A/B by anchoring on the wrong time point or applying Δ to only one person — svaret often inverted.
entries.countermeasure: Skriv alla åldrar i termer av EN gemensam person + tid: 'idag = X', 'tidigare = X − Δ'. Skillnaderna är konstanta över tid; använd det.

entries.pattern_description: Quadratic with two roots — student picks one root and proceeds, missing that the other could give a different svaret.
entries.why_it_occurs: Once one root is found, the student stops solving. But x² = a (or x² − 2x − 3 = 0) has TWO roots; if the comparison depends on which root x equals, the answer may be D.
entries.common_distractor_signature: Picks A/B based on one root (often the positive one), missing the negative branch that flips the svaret → D.
entries.countermeasure: Vid kvadratisk ekvation: skriv ut BÅDA rötterna (faktorisera eller pq-formel). Om båda rötterna ger olika svar på frågan → D.

entries.pattern_description: Same area but different shape — student assumes same perimeter (or vice versa).
entries.why_it_occurs: Area and perimeter feel coupled ('större area = större omkrets'). They are not: for fixed area, the most efficient shape (circle/square) has the smallest perimeter; elongated shapes have larger perimeter.
entries.common_distractor_signature: Picks C (lika) when comparing a square's omkrets with a triangle's at same area, missing that the kvadrat has the smaller perimeter.
entries.countermeasure: Samma area ≠ samma omkrets. För given area: cirkel har minst omkrets, sedan kvadrat, sedan andra former. Räkna båda omkretsarna explicit.

entries.pattern_description: Reciprocal comparison — student forgets that for positive numbers, larger value gives smaller reciprocal.
entries.why_it_occurs: Reciprokens monotonicitet vänds (för positiva tal). Students conflate 'större a' with 'större 1/a'.
entries.common_distractor_signature: Picks A when B is correct (or vice versa), by transferring the order from a to 1/a directly.
entries.countermeasure: För positiva tal: 1/a är AVTAGANDE i a — större a ger mindre 1/a. (För negativa tal är 1/a också negativt; rita tallinje.)
entries.notes: TRAP-029 and TRAP-034 are related (both about 1/x monotonicity); kept separate because 029 is fraction-with-equal-täljare and 034 is reciprocal-of-a-number.

entries.pattern_description: Quadratic f(x) is identical at two non-trivial roots of f — student computes both values numerically without spotting that f(a) = f(b) = 0.
entries.why_it_occurs: Numerical evaluation feels safe but misses the structural shortcut: if a, b are both roots of f, then f(a) = f(b) (often 0). Students burn time and risk arithmetic errors.
entries.common_distractor_signature: Picks A or B due to a calculation slip, when factoring would have shown both quantities equal (C) instantly.
entries.countermeasure: Innan du räknar f(a) och f(b) direkt: faktorisera f. Om både a och b är nollställen ⇒ f(a) = f(b) = 0 → C.

entries.pattern_description: Integer constraint ('positiva heltal') — student treats it like 'positiva tal' and misses divisibility filters.
entries.why_it_occurs: 'Positiv' is the salient word; 'heltal' is dropped. But the integer condition forces divisibility — e.g. x + 2y = 54 with x, y positiva heltal restricts y to integer values, not 0.5 or 1.7.
entries.common_distractor_signature: Picks A/B by picking the algebraically smallest x, missing the divisibility floor that forces a larger minimum.
entries.countermeasure: Vid 'positiva heltal': räkna upp delbarhetsvillkoret. Vilka värden av variabeln ger heltalslösning för den andra? Det är dina tillåtna val.

entries.pattern_description: Substitution with |x| or unspecified sign of x — student picks one sign without testing the other.
entries.why_it_occurs: When the problem gives |x| = 3 or x² = 9, x can be +3 OR -3. Students take the positive branch silently and miss the negative case.
entries.common_distractor_signature: Picks A/B/C by computing with x = +3, missing that x = -3 may flip the comparison → D.
entries.countermeasure: När du har |x| eller x² i ett villkor: lista x = +värde och x = -värde explicit. Räkna båda; verdikt kan vara D.

entries.pattern_description: Percent multiplication is commutative (a% of b = b% of a) — student treats it as order-dependent.
entries.why_it_occurs: Procent feels like a one-way operation ('30 % av 80' is not visually symmetric with '80 % av 30'). Algebraically: a% · b = (a/100) · b = (b/100) · a = b% · a.
entries.common_distractor_signature: Picks A or B trying to compute both sides, missing that the values are identical by construction (C).
entries.countermeasure: a % av b = b % av a (alltid, eftersom multiplikation är kommutativ). Spotta den symmetrin innan du räknar — det är ofta direkt C.
